results.title: This FREE AI Tool Clones ANY Website with ONE ...
results.content:
What if you could point an AI agent at any website, type one command, and get a clean modern rebuild of it in minutes? That's the promise of the AI website cloner template, an open-source project that just blew past 24,000 GitHub stars. And I just tested it for real on my own machine. Here's the idea. It's not an app, and it's not a library. It's a template repository. You get a ready-made Next.js 16 project with React 19, Tailwind version 4, and Shadcn UI, plus one powerful {slash} command called clone website. Your AI coding agent does all the work. The pipeline runs in five phases. First, reconnaissance, where the agent screenshots the target and extracts design tokens. Then, foundation, fonts, colors, and assets. Then it writes detailed component specs with exact CSS values. Then, [...] # This FREE AI Tool Clones ANY Website with ONE Command (I Tested It)
## Prompt Engineer
26300 subscribers
16 likes

### Description
351 views
Posted: 3 Jul 2026
Get GPUs Runpod: 
Get CPU Hostinger:  

Point an AI coding agent at any website, run /clone-website, and get a clean Next.js 16 rebuild. That's the promise of the AI Website Cloner Template (24.9k+ GitHub stars). In this video I clone the template on my own machine, dig into the brilliant SKILL.md multi-agent architecture, and run a REAL test: rebuilding the Ollama homepage — real assets, real content, real code — in minutes.

Everything shown is a real run on my Windows laptop. No faked results. [...] Next.js codebase. You can now edit, restyle, and build on. What I like, it's free and MIT licensed. The multi-agent architecture is genuinely clever. Small tasks mean perfect results. It works with basically every AI coding tool, Claude Code, Cursor, Co-pilot, WinSurf, and more. The output is a clean modern stack, and the skill file itself is a masterclass in agent prompting. Now, the honest downsides, it's not standalone. You need an AI coding agent, and long clone runs burn tokens. It needs Node 24 to be fully supported. Browser automation is required. No browser tool, no clone. It only clones the front end, no back end, no database. Complex animated sites will still challenge it. And obviously, respect copyright and terms of service. This is for learning and migration, not ripping off

results.title: Best website cloning tools for effortlessly copying ...
results.content:
### Comparing pricing models and value for money

Choosing the right website cloning tool depends on your specific needs and technical expertise.

If you’re looking for a free and simple solution for Windows, WebCopy by Cyotek is a solid choice.

HTTrack is the best free method for pro users.

For command-line enthusiasts who prefer power and flexibility, Wget is an excellent open-source alternative.

On the AI side, 10Web AI stands out for its automation capabilities, especially for Elementor users.

Nelio Unlocker and Nimbufy cater to specific platforms like Elementor and Oxygen, respectively, making them ideal for users within those ecosystems. [...] ##### Pricing Options

HTTrack is a free, open-source tool, making it highly accessible for individuals and businesses alike. Unlike many other web cloning tools that operate on a subscription basis or offer limited free trials, HTTrack allows users to take advantage of its full capabilities without any cost.

This makes it an attractive option for budget-conscious users who still require robust functionality.

##### Key Features

HTTrack boasts a range of features designed to simplify the website cloning process: [...] 1-top-website-cloning-tools-for-copying-web-designs-httrack

##### Pros/Cons

###### Pros

Cost-Effective: Being free makes HTTrack an excellent option for users with varying budgets.

Powerful and Versatile: With numerous customization options, it can adapt to different needs, from simple downloads to complex site structures.

Strong Community Support: As an open-source tool, HTTrack has a large user community, providing support and resources through forums and documentation.

###### Cons

The interface can be a bit outdated and overwhelming for beginners.

Limited Technical Support: Being an open-source tool, users may have to rely on community forums for assistance rather than dedicated customer service.

results.title: Instagram
results.content:
This Free Tool Can Clone Any Website  
  
Building websites from scratch is becoming optional.  
  
One open-source tool is making it incredibly easy to learn from existing websites.  
  
Here’s how it works:  
  
• Paste a website URL  
• Download the page locally  
• Explore its structure and layout  
• Use it as inspiration for your own project  
  
Then comes the real workflow.  
  
Open the downloaded project in Cursor, Claude Code, or your favorite AI coding tool.  
  
Ask it to:  
  
• Change the branding  
• Rewrite the copy  
• Replace the colors  
• Add new sections  
• Make it unique  
  
In minutes, you have a customized starting point.  
  
The biggest shift isn’t cloning websites.  
  
It’s eliminating the blank page.

results.title: Best AI Website Cloners Compared (2026, Honest Review)
results.content:
## So which one should you use?

The honest answer depends on your source:

 The design is a live website: extract the real code. Don't screenshot it and ask an AI to guess what's already in the browser. MiroMiro is the cleanest fit, and the only one here without a recurring bill.
 The design is only a screenshot or mockup, no live source: an AI cloner is reasonable. The open-source screenshot-to-code project or Builder.io will rebuild an approximation you then clean up.
 You want to archive a whole site offline: HTTrack. It's free and built for exactly that.
 You're building something new from a description: v0, Lovable, or Bolt. There's no source to clone, so generation is the right call. [...] Do this on any live website

MiroMiro inspects any site in your browser - CSS, assets, colors, and clean code, one click away.

Add to Chrome - free →

## Full-site downloaders (mirror the raw files)

A different category that often gets lumped under "cloning." These don't use AI at all. They save the files.

### 4. HTTrack

What it does: The classic open-source website copier. Point it at a URL and it crawls and downloads every reachable page and asset into a static mirror you can browse offline (httrack.com).

Output quality: Byte-for-byte, which sounds great until you open it. You get the raw shipped files: minified CSS, framework data attributes, every asset whether you want it or not. It's a mirror, not reusable components. [...] Share:

 Building this into a product? Website-to-code API → Turn any live section into Tailwind, JSX or Vue - programmatically. 100 free credits a month, no card.
